description: Study 4a/4a': memorization and reasoning-depth control. 4a: state the answer from memory, no tools (6 problems x 3 runs). 4a': same but multi-paragraph chain-of-thought allowed, still no tools.
motivation: Test whether Study 3's null is just a no-headroom corpus: if bare recall already solves everything no tool can show value; if prose CoT alone matches forced-viz, the variable is reasoning depth, not the tool.
formal_results: Bare single-shot recall is unreliable (~30%; e.g. 1993.A1 gave 4/9, 2/9, 3/2 across runs). Prose chain-of-thought with NO tools -> 18/18 (100%), matching forced-viz. The 4a' header records a ~30% -> ~80% answer-correctness gap between bare recall and the tool-mediated condition.
results_description: The model did not reliably know the answers (so Study 3's null is not pure memorization), but prose CoT alone reaches 100% - multi-turn reasoning, not the tool, drives the solve rate.
conclusions: Reasoning depth, not tool availability, is the dominant variable, and this corpus has no headroom for any tool. Corollary: tool evaluations on a CoT-saturated corpus cannot detect any tool's value.
